tool.name: DotMatch CRISPR count
tool.description: assign CRISPR guide reads to a known guide library
tool.command:
printf 'sample_id	fastq
%s	%s
%s	%s
' \ '$sample1_label' '$sample1_fastq' \ '$sample2_label' '$sample2_fastq' \ > samples.tsv
dotmatch crispr-count \ --library '$library' \ --samples samples.tsv \ --guide-start '$guide_start' \ --guide-length '$guide_length' \ --k '$k' \ --metric '$metric' \ #if str($metric) == 'levenshtein' and int($indel_window) > 0 --indel-window '$indel_window' \ #end if --out '$counts' \ --summary '$summary' \ --sample-qc '$sample_qc' \ --ambiguous '$ambiguous'

param.name: library
param.label: Guide library
param.help: MAGeCK-style CSV or target TSV accepted by DotMatch.

param.name: guide_start
param.label: Guide start
param.help: Zero-based start position of the guide window in each read.

param.name: indel_window
param.label: Levenshtein indel window
param.help: Used only when metric is Levenshtein.

tool.help:
DotMatch assigns short reads to a known CRISPR guide library and writes a MAGeCK-compatible count table. Assignments are deterministic: ambiguous reads are not silently counted for a target.
DotMatch is a known-target short-DNA assignment engine, not a genome aligner. It does not produce SAM/BAM, CIGAR strings, or reference mapping output.
